question: Using the Pittsburgh neighborhoods in the neighborhood profile, which neighborhood is the only one to appear in all three 1984 top-ten lists for share of college graduates, median household income, and share of workers employed within Pittsburgh? Among Pittsburgh neighborhoods that, in 2010, had populations no greater than that neighborhood's 1984 population, owner-occupied housing-unit percentages no higher than that neighborhood's 1984 percentage, and estimated poverty rates below 5 percent, which had the second-highest number of residents enrolled in grades 1 through 12 in 2015? As of 2025, how many Pittsburgh neighborhoods and boroughs border that neighborhood? Please return only the number in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import os
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.client import Config

bucket = 'lakeqa-yc4103-datalake'
source = 'datagov/a-community-profile-of-pittsburgh-neighborhoods-1974/files/arrests-for-major-crimes-1972.txt'
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else '/tmp/k123_data_cache/' + source
client = boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1', config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, dtype=str, low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = client.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj['Body'].read()), dtype=str, low_memory=False)

df['neighborhood'] = df['neighborhood'].astype(str).str.strip()
answer = '; '.join(sorted(df['neighborhood'].dropna().unique().tolist()))
1.subquestion: In this Pittsburgh neighborhood profile, which neighborhood names appear? Return them as a semicolon-separated list sorted alphabetically.
1.answer: 31st Ward; Allegheny Center; Allegheny West; Allentown; Arlington; Arlington Heights; Banksville; Bedford Dwellings; Beechview; Beltzhoover; Bloomfield; Bon Air; Brighton Heights; Brookline; California Ave.; Carrick; Central North Side; Crafton Heights - Westwood - Oakwood; East Carnegie; East Hills; East Liberty; East North Side; Elliot; Esplen; Friendship; Garfield; Greenfield; Harpen Hilltop; Hazelwood-Glenwood-Glen Hazel; Highland Park; Homewood North; Homewood South; Homewood West; Knoxville; Larimer; Lawrenceville; Lincoln-Lemington-Belmar; Lower Lawrenceville; Lower Oakland; Manchester; Middle Hill; Morningside; Mount Washington - Duquesne Heights; North Oakland; North View Heights; Perry North; Perry South; Point Breeze; Polish Hill; Regent Square; Ridgemont - Chicken Hill; Shadeland Halls Grove; Shadyside; Sheraton Chartiers; South Oakland; Southside Flats; Southside Slopes; Spring Hill; Squirrel Hill - North of Forbes; Squirrel Hill - South of Forbes; St. Clair; Stanton Heights; Swisshelm Park; Terrace Village; The Bluff; The Strip; Troy Hill; Upper Hill; Upper Lawrenceville; West End Valley

import io
import os
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.client import Config

bucket = 'lakeqa-yc4103-datalake'
source = 'datagov/1984-community-profiles-a-descriptive-picture-of-communities-in-allegheny-county/files/civics.txt'

client = boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1', config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, dtype=str, low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = client.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj['Body'].read()), dtype=str, low_memory=False)

node_1_answer = '31st Ward; Allegheny Center; Allegheny West; Allentown; Arlington; Arlington Heights; Banksville; Bedford Dwellings; Beechview; Beltzhoover; Bloomfield; Bon Air; Brighton Heights; Brookline; California Ave.; Carrick; Central North Side; Crafton Heights - Westwood - Oakwood; East Carnegie; East Hills; East Liberty; East North Side; Elliot; Esplen; Friendship; Garfield; Greenfield; Harpen Hilltop; Hazelwood-Glenwood-Glen Hazel; Highland Park; Homewood North; Homewood South; Homewood West; Knoxville; Larimer; Lawrenceville; Lincoln-Lemington-Belmar; Lower Lawrenceville; Lower Oakland; Manchester; Middle Hill; Morningside; Mount Washington - Duquesne Heights; North Oakland; North View Heights; Perry North; Perry South; Point Breeze; Polish Hill; Regent Square; Ridgemont - Chicken Hill; Shadeland Halls Grove; Shadyside; Sheraton Chartiers; South Oakland; Southside Flats; Southside Slopes; Spring Hill; Squirrel Hill - North of Forbes; Squirrel Hill - South of Forbes; St. Clair; Stanton Heights; Swisshelm Park; Terrace Village; The Bluff; The Strip; Troy Hill; Upper Hill; Upper Lawrenceville; West End Valley'
allowed = set(node_1_answer.split('; '))
df['Community'] = df['Community'].astype(str).str.strip()
df['%grad_coll'] = pd.to_numeric(df['%grad_coll'].astype(str).str.replace('%', '', regex=False).str.strip(), errors='coerce')
filtered = df[df['Community'].isin(allowed) & df['%grad_coll'].notna()].copy()
ranked = filtered[['Community', '%grad_coll']].drop_duplicates().sort_values(['%grad_coll', 'Community'], ascending=[False, True]).head(10)
answer = '; '.join(ranked['Community'].tolist())
2.subquestion: Among the neighborhoods listed in node 1, which ten rank highest by share of college graduates in 1984? Return them as a semicolon-separated list in rank order.
2.answer: Point Breeze; Regent Square; Shadyside; Highland Park; Allegheny Center; Friendship; Banksville; Stanton Heights; East Liberty; Greenfield
